title: Expressions, Decisions, and Iteration · Spiral Review
summary: Route support cases through explicit rules and iterate changing record sets without truthiness or termination defects. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a transparent support-case routing engine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict expression grouping, short-circuit evaluation, assignment, optional chaining, and nullish coalescing.
Use truthiness only when every falsy value shares the same domain meaning and otherwise write explicit guards.
Express mutually exclusive branches, guard clauses, and exhaustive fallbacks without unreachable paths.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Operator Precedence · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — Route tickets with priority 6, role "viewer", and maintenance=false through an explicit decision table. Then flip maintenance to true and prove exactly one branch owns the changed outcome.

Which action demonstrates Operator Precedence without dropping earlier contracts?

content.title: Evidence defense
